description: Trigger discrimination for black-box production monitoring (this skill) vs pre-release load/perf measurement (performance-test-harness / load-test-planner), CI end-to-end tests (playwright-e2e-engineer), SLO/SLI target-setting (slo-reliability-architect), and white-box internal instrumentation (observability-operator).

cases.prompt: Design scheduled probes that exercise our critical production journeys and third-party dependencies from the outside, alert us before customers notice, and never touch real data unsafely.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary) — scheduled external probes of production journeys/dependencies with a prod-safety contract and alerting is black-box synthetic monitoring. Not a pre-release load test, not a CI gate, not internal telemetry.
cases.reason: Ongoing prod-safe external probing that watches whether production works from the user's path is this skill; it feeds SLOs and complements white-box telemetry rather than being either.

cases.prompt: Before we launch, model realistic peak traffic across tenants and data volumes and measure where latency and throughput degrade so we know our capacity.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (reverse) — modeling peak load to find the degradation point is pre-release MEASUREMENT (with performance-test-harness for instrumentation). Synthetic monitoring watches production on a schedule after ship.
cases.reason: Pre-release capacity/latency measurement under modeled load is load-test-planner/performance-test-harness. Synthetic monitoring is post-ship watching of the live system, not a load test.

cases.prompt: Build the end-to-end browser tests that run in CI against the preview environment and block a PR if the core flows break.
cases.reason: CI E2E tests on ephemeral preview data gating PRs are playwright-e2e-engineer. Synthetic monitoring is scheduled prod-safe probing of production, not a pre-merge CI gate; the two differ on target (prod vs preview) and safety envelope.

cases.prompt: Define our availability and latency SLOs and the error budget for each service — what we commit to and how we measure and spend it.
cases.reason: Setting the SLO/SLI targets and error budgets is slo-reliability-architect. Synthetic monitoring produces a synthetic SLI that FEEDS those targets; it does not define them.

cases.prompt: Instrument the application with metrics, distributed traces, and structured logs from inside, and wire the dashboards and alerting on those internal signals.
cases.reason: Internal white-box instrumentation (metrics/traces/logs from inside the app) is observability-operator. Synthetic monitoring is the external black-box complement that probes from the user's vantage point; the two are paired, not the same.
